org.arakhne.neteditor.io.eps

Class EpsUtil

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arakhne.neteditor.io.eps.EpsUtil

  •  class EpsUtil
extends Object
    Utilities for the EPS exporter.

    This graphic context supports the Postscript Reference Document Third Edition, and the EPS Reference Document 3.0.

    Since:
    0.4
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:03:04
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-io

      EpsUtil() 

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      static Point2D toEps(float x, float y)
      Convert the given point from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.
      static String toEps(Image image, int sx1, int sy1, int sx2, int sy2)
      Convert the given image into a PDF sequence of bytes.
      static Point2D toEps(Point2D point)
      Convert the given point from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.
      static Rectangle2f toEps(Rectangle2f bounds)
      Convert the given rectangle from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.
      static String toEps(String text)
      Convert a string into a EPS text by escaping special charaters.
      static float toEpsAngle(float a)
      Convert the given angle from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.
      static float toEpsX(float x)
      Convert the given coordinate along the x axis from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.
      static float toEpsY(float y)
      Convert the given coordinate along the y axis from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.
      static float toEpsYInverted(float y)
      Convert the given coordinate along the y axis from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space -y.

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • EpsUtil

        EpsUtil()

    • Method Detail

      • toEpsX

        public static float toEpsX(float x)
        Convert the given coordinate along the x axis from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.

        The standard coordinate system has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive downward. The EPS user space has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive upward.

        Parameters:
        x -
        Returns:
        the x in the EPS user space.

      • toEpsY

        public static float toEpsY(float y)
        Convert the given coordinate along the y axis from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.

        The standard coordinate system has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive downward. The EPS user space has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive upward.

        Parameters:
        y -
        Returns:
        the y in the EPS user space.

      • toEpsYInverted

        public static float toEpsYInverted(float y)
        Convert the given coordinate along the y axis from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space -y.

        The standard coordinate system has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive downward. The EPS user space has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive upward.

        Parameters:
        y -
        Returns:
        the y in the EPS user space.

      • toEps

        public static Rectangle2f toEps(Rectangle2f bounds)
        Convert the given rectangle from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.

        The standard coordinate system has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive downward. The EPS user space has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive upward.

        Parameters:
        bounds - are the bounds to translate.
        Returns:
        the bounds in the EPS user space.

      • toEps

        public static Point2D toEps(Point2D point)
        Convert the given point from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.

        The standard coordinate system has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive downward. The EPS user space has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive upward.

        Parameters:
        point - is the point to translate.
        Returns:
        the point in the EPS user space.

      • toEps

        public static Point2D toEps(float x,
            float y)
        Convert the given point from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.

        The standard coordinate system has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive downward. The EPS user space has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive upward.

        Parameters:
        x - is the x coordinate of the point to translate.
        y - is the y coordinate of the point to translate.
        Returns:
        the point in the EPS user space.

      • toEps

        public static String toEps(String text)
        Convert a string into a EPS text by escaping special charaters.
        Parameters:
        text - is the text to convert.
        Returns:
        the EPS text.

      • toEps

        public static String toEps(Image image,
           int sx1,
           int sy1,
           int sx2,
           int sy2)
                    throws IOException
        Convert the given image into a PDF sequence of bytes.
        Parameters:
        image -
        sx1 - is the x coordinate of the first corner of the subimage to extract.
        sy1 - is the y coordinate of the first corner of the subimage to extract.
        sx2 - is the x coordinate of the second corner of the subimage to extract.
        sy2 - is the y coordinate of the second corner of the subimage to extract.
        Returns:
        the sequence of bytes to put in the EPS document.
        Throws:
        IOException

      • toEpsAngle

        public static float toEpsAngle(float a)
        Convert the given angle from the standard vectorial coordinate system to the EPS user space.

        The standard coordinate system has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive downward. The EPS user space has the x axis positive to the right and the y axis positive upward.

        Parameters:
        a - is the angle to translate.
        Returns:
        the angle in the EPS user space.
